title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: America’s national debt borrowing binge means interest payments will rocket to $2 trillion a year by 2036, CBO says
items.description: Annual interest payments will double to $2.14 trillion by 2036, nearly double the yearly budget for spending on defense.

items.title: U.S. Health Officials Defend Rejection of Moderna’s Flu Vaccine
items.description: The F.D.A.’s refusal to examine the company’s mRNA shot drew widespread criticism from doctors and was divisive within the agency.

items.title: Military’s Use of Anti-Drone Technology Said to Cause El Paso Airspace Closure
items.description: Federal Aviation Administration officials said privately that the agency did not have enough time or information to assess the technology’s risk to co...

items.title: Instagram Chief Says Social Media Is Not ‘Clinically Addictive’ in Landmark Trial
items.description: Adam Mosseri, who leads the Meta-owned app, testified that the company was careful to test features used by young people before releasing them.

items.title: Quiz: How Much Do You Know About the Winter Olympics?
items.description: As top athletes prepare to test their limits in Milan and Cortina d’Ampezzo, why not take a minute to test your knowledge of Winter Olympics trivia?

items.title: At 22, Olympic skier Eileen Gu is worth over $20 million —she’s juggling brand deals and sports with school. And she urges Gen Z not to wait until they’re ‘older’ to start.
items.description: 22-year-old Olympian Eileen Gu is the fourth-highest-paid woman in sports, ahead of stars like Naomi Osaka and Caitlin Clark. Yet she’s still a studen...

items.title: The Gen Z job nightmare is so bad that even billionaires are worried their kids won’t be able to keep a job, says wealth advisor to the 0.1%
items.description: It’s a “very real concern” for the ultrawealthy that their children aren’t prepared for the future, wealth adviser Tom Thiegs told Fortune.

items.title: ‘The fiscal trajectory is not sustainable’: CBO warns about the highest debt in U.S. history as Trump adds $1.4 trillion to 10-year deficit
items.description: The national debt is set to surpass the historic high of 106% of GDP (which it reached in 1946) by 2030, according to the CBO.

items.title: Big Oil embraces global exploration again as Chevron returns to Libya
items.description: American Big Oil is returning to countries in the Middle East, Africa, and beyond to find new sources of oil and gas.

items.title: James Van Der Beek, ‘Dawson’s Creek’ Actor, Dies at 48 After Cancer Battle
items.description: He first appeared in a hit TV drama as a wide-eyed 15-year-old who then grew up over six seasons. He announced he had cancer in 2024.

items.title: How Hate Groups Are Using Online Games to Recruit Kids
items.description: Fringe movements are using games and other online platforms to draw growing numbers of children to their causes, new data and dozens of interviews sho...

items.title: Switzerland to Vote on Capping Population at 10 Million
items.description: After a campaign by a right-wing opposition party, the government will hold a referendum in June that would require measures to limit immigration.

items.title: U.K. Police Weigh Whether to Formally Investigate Former Prince Andrew Over Epstein Accusations

items.title: Live Updates: Police Identify Suspect in Mass Shooting in Canada
items.description: The 18-year-old fatally shot her mother and stepbrother as well as six people at a school, before killing herself. Her motive was unclear, but police ...
